calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Dec-17-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: none.

There being no appearances, the court takes the Petition for Letters of Administration off calendar.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Nov-05-2024 Continued To Dec-17-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-03-2024 Continued To Nov-05-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Yasue Yoshioka in propria persona.
Mena D'Urzo (Yasue's daughter) in propria persona.

Mrs. Yoshioka stated that they used a small estate affidavit. Mrs. Urzo stated that her mother attempted to receive assistance from the bar association, that no one wanted to take the case, that they had the small estate affidavit notarized and that they did proof of publication. Mrs. Urzo also stated that they took the affidavit to the bank, that the bank told them that they had to come to court, that the life insurance policy was her grandmother's, that half went half to her mother and the other half to her deceased aunt and that the insurance company issued a check in the amount of $33,562.86 payable to the deceased aunt's name. The court noted that there are a number of issues with the petition that need to be fixed, suggested that they call the probate examiner, informed the parties that notes were e-mailed to Mrs. Yoshioka in May and explained the difficulty that arises in the aunt being a resident of Japan. The parties will contact the insurance company to see if the check can be re-issued in Mrs. Yoshioka's name and try going to other banks to see if they will cash the check. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to November 5, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Lopez. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-05-2024 Continued To May-15-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Yasue Yoshioka, in person, in propria persona, Petitioner.

At the outset, the court confirms with Petitioner that she has not arranged for publication and that the lone asset of the estate is an insurance benefit in the amount stated in the petition. The court indicates that Petitioner may want to consider obtaining a small estate affidavit under Probate Code 13100 or consult with an attorney for assistance with this matter. Petitioner is provided with contact information for the San Francisco Bar Association.

The subject matter is continued to May-15-2024 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
